title: Gyeran Mari Korean Rolled Omelette

seo.title: How to Make Gyeran Mari Korean Rolled Omelette — Easy Korean Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make authentic Gyeran Mari Korean Rolled Omelette. This easy Korean recipe includes step-by-step instructions, tips, and serving ideas.

easy Gyeran Mari Korean Rolled Omelette recipe
authentic Korean Gyeran Mari Korean Rolled Omelette

seo.imageAlt: Authentic Korean Gyeran Mari Korean Rolled Omelette recipe
seo.imageCaption: Delicious homemade Gyeran Mari Korean Rolled Omelette — a beloved Korean classic

description: Gyeran Mari is a popular Korean side dish made from eggs, rolled into a beautiful, layered omelette. It is often enjoyed as part of a breakfast spread or packed into lunchboxes. Its delicate flavor and appealing presentation make it a beloved dish for all ages.

ingredients.notes: optional, for a hint of sweetness

instructions.title: Prepare the Egg Mixture
instructions.text: In a medium bowl, crack the large eggs. Add the finely diced carrot, finely chopped green onions, and salt. If desired, add a pinch of sugar for a subtle sweetness. Whisk everything together until well combined, but avoid over-whisking to prevent too many air bubbles.

instructions.title: Heat the Pan
instructions.text: Heat a non-stick rectangular or round pan over medium-low heat. Lightly brush the pan with a small amount of cooking oil. It is important to keep the heat low to prevent the omelette from browning too quickly.

instructions.title: Pour the First Layer
instructions.text: Pour about one-third of the egg mixture into the hot pan, tilting the pan to spread it evenly into a thin layer that covers the bottom. Cook for about 1-2 minutes until the edges are set and the top is still slightly wet.

instructions.title: Start Rolling the Omelette
instructions.text: Using a spatula, gently start rolling the cooked egg layer from one end to the other, forming a tight roll. Push the rolled egg to one side of the pan, leaving space for the next layer.

instructions.title: Add Subsequent Layers
instructions.text: Brush a tiny bit more oil onto the empty part of the pan if needed. Pour another one-third of the egg mixture into the pan, making sure some of it flows underneath the already rolled egg. Cook until set, then roll the existing omelette over the new layer, continuing to form a larger roll. Repeat this process with the remaining egg mixture.

instructions.title: Finish and Slice
instructions.text: Once all the egg mixture is used and you have a thick roll, cook for another minute or two, gently pressing all sides to ensure it is cooked through and lightly golden. Remove the Gyeran Mari from the pan and let it cool on a cutting board for a few minutes before slicing into 1-inch thick pieces. Serve warm.

tips: Maintain a low heat throughout the cooking process. This prevents the omelette from browning too quickly and ensures even cooking, resulting in a soft, moist texture.
For extra flavor and color, consider adding finely chopped bell peppers or ham. You can also add a splash of milk to the egg mixture for a creamier texture.
Gyeran Mari is best served warm, but it can also be enjoyed cold, making it perfect for lunchboxes or picnics. Store leftovers in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 2-3 days.
article: Gyeran Mari Korean Rolled Omelette is a beloved Korean Breakfast Recipes dish enjoyed across Korean. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
